title: ochna bracteosa
intro: Ochna bracteosa, also known as the small-laalaa or Mickey mouse plant, is a small, evergreen shrub native to the eastern and southern parts of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ochna bracteosa include its use in treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Ochna bracteosa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves, to treat various health conditions.
